title: Medical Latin Adjectives — Core Set I (LA → EN)

items.hint: used for structures, pain, walls, or organs of the abdomen
describes outward movement; e.g. abducens nerve moves the eye laterally
used for lesions or processes that develop into an abscess
describes inward movement of a limb or structure
used for pain, angles, or diseases with rapid onset

items.translation: related to a socket or small cavity
items.hint: used for tooth sockets or pulmonary alveoli
used for open wounds, open procedures, or patent passages

items.translation: related to the tip, summit, or apex
items.hint: used for lung apex, root apex, cardiac apex
used for artificial airway, artificial feeding, induced conditions
used for arterial blood, arterial wall, arterial supply
used for auditory pathways, auditory canal, auditory testing

items.translation: affected by low blood or low hemoglobin state
items.hint: used for patients or tissue states associated with anemia
used for tumors or clinical courses with favorable behavior
common in muscle names to distinguish from longus

items.translation: having two heads or two points of origin
items.hint: used especially for muscles such as biceps brachii

items.translation: able to hold much; spacious; with large capacity
items.hint: used for cavities or reservoirs with expanded capacity
may refer to neck structures or the cervix depending on context
used for diseases with prolonged course
used for communicating branches, vessels, or spaces
used for rashes, lesions, or vessels that join into one area
used for malformations, syndromes, and structural abnormalities existing from birth
used for ligaments, sutures, and anatomical patterns
used for nutrient, hormone, or functional deficiency states
used for diseases that progressively alter normal structure
used for right organs, limbs, chambers, and directional anatomy

items.translation: triangular; shaped like the Greek letter delta
items.hint: used for the deltoid muscle and similar shapes

items.translation: moving apart; spreading away from a common point
items.hint: used for strabismus, vessels, or fibers that separate
used to distinguish external from internal layers or structures
used for facial nerve, facial muscles, facial bones

items.translation: made of fibers; fibrous; scar-like in texture
items.hint: used for capsules, connective tissue, thickened bands
used for papillae, appendages, or narrow projections

items.translation: related to the forehead or front part
items.hint: used for frontal bone, frontal lobe, frontal sinus
classically used for fungiform papillae of the tongue
used for hepatic artery, hepatic veins, hepatic disease
used for early disease or initial pathological change

items.translation: occurring on and off; not continuous
items.hint: used for fever, symptoms, pulse, or obstruction that comes and goes
used for threatened rupture, labor, or other urgent events

items.translation: not suitable for surgery; cannot be operated on
items.hint: used for tumors or conditions that are unresectable
used to distinguish internal from external structures

items.translation: not stable; prone to change or collapse
items.hint: used for joints, fractures, hemodynamics, mood, gait
used for juvenile forms of disease
used for mild symptoms or slight intensity
common in anatomical naming to distinguish from brevis

items.translation: related to lymph or lymph vessels
items.hint: used for lymphatic drainage, vessels, and nodes
used in anatomy for larger paired structures
used for tumors with invasion, metastasis, or dangerous progression

items.translation: done by hand; relating to the hand
items.hint: used for manual examination, manipulation, or hand-related actions

items.translation: located at the edge or border
items.hint: used for margins of organs, teeth, lesions, or surgical fields
used for median plane, median nerve, midline structures
directional anatomical term opposite of lateralis
